mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear months with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and capital expenditure cycles, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion phase reflects strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days via profit margin expansion.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion phases, with a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth consistently coincides with positive 5-day SPX returns, or the predictive slope is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized confirms sustained aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in >65% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate validate a broad-based labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates weakening manufacturing demand and impending corporate earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases consistently precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy falls below 50%.
